chapter.title: Overview
chapter.simpara: The Apache Kafka plugin connects QALIPSIS to an Apache Kafka cluster.

listitem.simpara: Kafka: https://kafka.apache.org/
Kafka: refer to Apache Kafka
consume: consumes data from topics of an Apache Kafka cluster.
produce: pushes data to topics of the Apache Kafka cluster.

chapter.simpara: An example scenario that uses a combination of the kafka consume and produce steps is provided on GitHub.

chapter.title: Consume step
chapter.simpara: The consume step within the Apache Kafka plugin polls data from topics of an Apache Kafka cluster and forwards the data to the next step, either in batches or individually.

listitem.simpara: is generally used in conjunction with a left join to assert data or inject data into a workflow.
supports multithreading (concurrent consumers) using the concurrency property.
Scenario

The consume step starts a polling loop for the designated client, using the provided subscription topics name, or topicsPattern and groupId. When new records appear, consume polls them and sends them to the next step(s).

listitem.programlisting: kafka().consume { bootstrap("localhost:9092") topics("battery_state") groupId("kafka-example") offsetReset(OffsetResetStrategy.EARLIEST) pollTimeout(Duration.ofSeconds(1)) }

listitem.simpara: bootstrap: configures the bootstrap hosts of the Kafka cluster; defaults to localhost:9092.
groupId: defines the consumer group.
pollTimeout: defines the consumer poll timeout to wait for records; defaults to 1 second.
offsetReset (required): defines the strategy to apply when the consumer starts consuming messages from the queue.
pollDelay (required): specifies the delay between query executions.
maxPolledRecords (optional): defines the maximum number of records returned in a unique poll batch; defaults to 500.
broadcast (optional): specifies the broadcast parameters for the step.
Use the consume step with a left join to assert data or inject data into a workflow.
Valid offsetReset strategies are:
OffsetResetStrategy.EARLIEST: starts consuming messages from the beginning of the queue.
OffsetResetStrategy.LATEST: starts consuming messages when the consumer starts, ignoring all previous messages in the queue.
OffsetResetStrategy.LATEST is the default.
Refer to the Apache KAFKA documentation for further parameter and configuration information.

chapter.title: Produce step
chapter.simpara: The produce step within the Apache Kafka plugin pushes data onto topics of the Kafka broker and forwards the data as input to the next step.

listitem.simpara: Scenario
The produce step’s input and step context are used to generate values to complete the step. The batch of results, or a single result, is then forwarded to the next step(s).
keySerializer: describes how to serialize the record keys to send to kafka.
valueSerializer: describes how to serialize the record values to send to kafka.
bootstrap: configures the bootstrap hosts of the Kafka cluster; defaults to localhost:9092.
clientName: defines the name of the kafka client
records (required): generates and configures a list of produced records.
Refer to the Apache KAFKA documentation for further parameter and configuration information.

chapter.title: Configuration of publication of events and meters to Apache Kafka

listitem.simpara: enabled: boolean flag that activates/deactivates event publishing to Kafka; defaults to false; must be set to true.
minLevel: minimal accepted level of events; defaults to INFO; allowable values are `EventLevel`values: TRACE, DEBUG, INFO, WARN, ERROR, OFF.
min-level: minimal accepted level of events; defaults to INFO; allowable values are `EventLevel`values: TRACE, DEBUG, INFO, WARN, ERROR, OFF.
bootstrap: bootstraps (string of host and port pair) of the Kafka broker; defaults to localhost:9092.
topic: string name of the topic to write the events to; defaults to qalipsis-events.
durationAsNano: boolean flag that activates/deactivates converting the duration to nanoseconds; defaults to false (converts to milliseconds).
lingerPeriod: maximum period between two publications of events to Kafka; positive duration; defaults to 10s.
batchSize: maximum number of events buffered between two publications of events to Kafka; positive integer; defaults to 2.
duration-as-nano: boolean flag that activates/deactivates converting the duration to nanoseconds; defaults to false (converts to milliseconds).
linger-period: maximum period between two publications of events to Kafka; positive duration; defaults to 10s.
batch-size: maximum number of events buffered between two publications of events to Kafka; positive integer; defaults to 2.
configuration: key, value configuration parameters of type Properties, for the Kafka producer.
enabled: boolean flag that activates/deactivates meter publishing to Kafka; defaults to false; must be set to true.
step: The step size (reporting frequency); defaults to PT10S.
bootstrap.servers: bootstraps (string of host and port pair) of the Kafka broker; defaults to localhost:9092.
topic: string name of the topic to write events to; defaults to qalipsis-meters.
linger.ms: maximum period between two batches of events; positive duration; defaults to 5000.
batchSize: maximum number of events buffered between two publications of events to Kafka; positive integer; defaults to 800000 (in bytes).
batch-size: maximum number of events buffered between two publications of events to Kafka; positive integer; defaults to 800000 (in bytes).
delivery.timeout.ms: maximum integer value in milliseconds before time out.; defaults to 10000.
configuration: key, value configuration parameters of type Properties, for the Kafka producer.

section.title: Example
section.programlisting:
events: export: kafka: enabled: true bootstrap`: localhost:9092 topic: qalipsis-events duration-as-nano: false linger-period: 10s batch-size: 2 configuration: delivery.timeout.ms: 10000
meters: export: kafka: enabled: true step: PT10S bootstrap.servers: localhost:9092 topic: qalipsis-meters linger.ms: 5000 batch-size: 800000 # In bytes. configuration: delivery.timeout.ms: 10000
